title: solanum verbascifolium var. auriculatum
intro: Solanum verbascifolium var. auriculatum, also known as the auricled or ear-leaved solanum, is a plant species within the Solanaceae family, characterized by its distinctive auriculated leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly in Asia, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits associated with Solanum verbascifolium var. auriculatum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and aid in wound healing, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its adaptogenic and tonic properties.
